 {"id":"caam-cfb","title":"Usage analytics CLI command","description":"## Purpose
Add `caam usage` command to display profile usage statistics from the command line.

## Background
Users should be able to see their usage patterns to understand which profiles
they use most, identify underutilized accounts, and optimize their workflow.

## Command Structure
```bash
# Summary view (default)
caam usage

# Detailed view for specific profile
caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed

# Time range filtering
caam usage --days 30
caam usage --since 2025-12-01

# Output formats
caam usage --json
caam usage --csv
```

## Example Output
```
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs
gemini/default       3 sessions    0.5 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed
Session History for claude/work
───────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
...

Total: 42 sessions, 18.5 hours over 7 days
Average session: 26 minutes
Most active day: Monday (12 sessions)
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// cmd/caam/usage.go
var usageCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "usage",
    Short: "Display profile usage statistics",
    RunE:  runUsage,
}

func init() {
    usageCmd.Flags().StringP("profile", "p", "", "Profile to show (provider/name)")
    usageCmd.Flags().Bool("detailed", false, "Show detailed session history")
    usageCmd.Flags().Int("days", 7, "Number of days to include")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("format", "table", "Output format: table, json, csv")
}

func runUsage(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    db, err := db.Open()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer db.Close()
    
    // Query and format based on flags
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/usage.go
- cmd/caam/usage_test.go
- Modify cmd/caam/root.go to add subcommand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary shows all profiles with session count and duration
- [ ] Detailed view shows individual sessions for a profile
- [ ] Time range filtering works correctly
- [ ] JSON and CSV export work
- [ ] Handles empty database gracefully
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large activity logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:53.646488393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam usage command with summary/detailed views and json/csv output.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-cfb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:57.057526979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting caam usage command: implement cmd/caam/cmd/usage.go with summary + detailed profile view, since/days filtering, and table/json/csv output using internal/db.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:06:53Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented  command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:45Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"(Follow-up) Implemented the caam usage command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ckk","title":"E2E: CLI command workflow tests","description":"End-to-end integration tests for CLI command workflows:

## Test Scenarios
1. `caam list` - List all profiles across providers
2. `caam backup <provider> <email>` - Backup current auth state
3. `caam restore <provider> <email>` - Restore saved profile
4. `caam delete <provider> <email>` - Delete a saved profile
5. `caam status` - Show current auth status for all providers

## Requirements
- Use t.TempDir() for isolated file system tests
- Real file operations (no mocks)
- Test actual cobra command execution
- Detailed logging at each step
- Test error cases (missing files, invalid profiles)

## Logging Format
Each test should log:
- [START] Test name and description
- [SETUP] Temp directory and fixture creation
- [EXEC] Command being executed
- [VERIFY] Expected vs actual results
- [CLEANUP] Resources cleaned up
- [PASS/FAIL] Final result with duration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:52:40.357727069-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T02:15:21.938011493-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 9 E2E tests for CLI command workflows: list, backup, restore, delete, status, clear, profile workflow, invalid tool error handling, multi-provider isolation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:54:53.822477208-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:55:26.082995053-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

(2) Global config Save should use an atomic temp+rename write like other config files.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:08.998565976-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:12:19.339544479-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:12:19.339544479-05:00","close_reason":"Global config Save uses atomic temp+rename; Claude/Gemini OAuth URL capture avoids shadowing and flushes after command exit.","labels":["browser","config","provider","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"caam-d8x","author":"ubuntu","text":"Made global config Save atomic (temp+rename) and fixed Claude/Gemini OAuth URL capture to avoid shadowing and flush after the command exits. go test ./... and go test -race ./... pass.","created_at":"2025-12-18T06:12:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-dmw","title":"Project management CLI commands","description":"## Purpose
Add CLI commands for managing project-profile associations.

## Commands

### caam project set
Associate current directory with a profile.
```bash
caam project set claude client-a@work.com
# Output: Associated /home/user/work/client-a with claude/client-a@work.com
```

### caam project list
Show all project associations.
```bash
caam project list
# Output:
# /home/user/work/client-a
#   claude: client-a@work.com
#   codex:  work-main
#
# /home/user/personal/blog
#   claude: personal@gmail.com
```

### caam project show
Show associations for current directory.
```bash
cd /home/user/work/client-a
caam project show
# Output:
# Project: /home/user/work/client-a
# Associations:
#   claude: client-a@work.com
#   codex:  work-main
```

### caam project remove
Remove association.
```bash
caam project remove claude
# Output: Removed claude association from /home/user/work/client-a
```

### caam project clear
Remove all associations for current directory.
```bash
caam project clear
# Output: Cleared all associations from /home/user/work/client-a
```

## Auto-activation Integration
When running `caam activate claude` without specifying a profile:
1. Check if CWD has an association
2. If yes, use that profile automatically
3. If no, use default or prompt user

```bash
cd /home/user/work/client-a
caam activate claude
# Output: Using project association: claude/client-a@work.com
# Activated.
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/project.go (project subcommand)
- cmd/caam/project_test.go
- cmd/caam/activate.go (add project check)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] project set associates current directory
- [ ] project list shows all associations
- [ ] project show displays current directory associations
- [ ] project remove deletes single provider association
- [ ] project clear removes all associations
- [ ] activate uses project association when available
- [ ] Clear error messages for edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:49.003391908-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:56:39.484397921-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:56:39.484397921-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `caam project` commands (set/list/show/remove/clear) backed by internal/project store, added project-aware `caam activate <tool>` (optional profile) using CWD association or default profile, and added tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-dmw","depends_on_id":"caam-ir7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:35.619607689-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"caam-dmw","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation of `caam project` commands (set/list/show/remove/clear) and project-aware `caam activate <tool>` that uses CWD association when present (and config allows). Will add tests + wire into root command.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:48:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-dt4","title":"Refresh/expiry: harden Claude+Gemini refresh and vault path probing","description":"## Problem

Deep review found a few correctness/safety issues in the Smart Profile Management refresh pipeline:

- `internal/refresh/refresh.go:refreshGemini` calls `health.ParseGeminiExpiry(vaultPath)` and then treats `info.Source` as a Google ADC file via `ReadADC(info.Source)`. For vault profiles, `info.Source` is typically `settings.json` (Gemini CLI), which is *not* ADC format. This makes `caam refresh gemini …` reliably fail and also makes refresh-on-activate noisy.
- `health.ParseClaudeExpiry(authDir)` assumes `authDir` is an XDG config root and probes `authDir/claude-code/auth.json`, but vault backups store Claude’s optional XDG file as flat `auth.json` under the profile dir (see README vault structure). This can cause expiry/refresh token discovery to miss valid vaulted auth.
- `health.ParseGeminiExpiry(authDir)` returns `ErrNoAuthFile` if `settings.json` is missing even if `oauth_credentials.json` exists; it should consider any supported auth file.
- Claude refresh handler still uses unverified endpoint/client_id placeholders (see caam-fwz close reason). We should avoid making network calls with refresh tokens unless endpoints are known-safe or explicitly configured.

## Goal

- Make refresh behavior safe by default (no accidental network calls to unverified endpoints).
- Make gemini/claude refresh behavior internally consistent with vault layout.
- Ensure CLI treats “unsupported/not configured” as skip (not failure) while preserving hard failures for real errors.

## Proposed Fix

1. Add `refresh.UnsupportedError` (wrapping a sentinel `refresh.ErrUnsupported`) and use it for:
   - Claude refresh when endpoint/client_id are not configured/verified
   - Gemini refresh when required credential file is missing/unsupported
2. Update `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go` to count `ErrUnsupported` as “skipped” instead of “failed”.
3. Update `health.ParseClaudeExpiry(authDir)` probe order when `authDir != ""`:
   - `authDir/.claude.json`
   - `authDir/auth.json` (vault flattened)
   - `authDir/claude-code/auth.json` (isolated profile layout)
4. Update `health.ParseGeminiExpiry(authDir)` so `ErrNoAuthFile` only when *none* of `settings.json` / `oauth_credentials.json` exist.
5. Update gemini refresh to read credentials from `oauth_credentials.json` (or other ADC-like file in vault) and update the appropriate auth file/health accordingly, otherwise return `ErrUnsupported`.

## Acceptance
- `caam refresh --all` does not fail just because Claude/Gemini refresh is unsupported; it reports skipped with reason.
- Unit tests cover new unsupported/skip behavior and vault path probing changes.

## Related
- caam-fwz (Claude refresh handler placeholders)
- caam-9os (Gemini refresh handler)
- caam-thb (expiry parsing)
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:16:21.041789816-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:37:46.538620938-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:37:46.538620938-05:00","close_reason":"Hardened refresh/expiry: endpoint allowlists, Gemini vault credentials+settings update, improved Claude/Gemini vault probing, unsupported treated as skip; tests added.","comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"caam-dt4","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Claimed. Investigating internal/refresh + internal/health path probing; will add explicit unsupported/not-configured error type and adjust CLI to treat it as skip, plus fix Gemini credential source selection and Claude vault auth.json probing.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:16:44Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"caam-dt4","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Implemented refresh/expiry hardening: added `refresh.ErrUnsupported` + `UnsupportedError` and taught `caam refresh` + activate refresh-on-activate to treat unsupported as skipped (not failed). Gemini refresh now reads ADC-like client creds from vault `oauth_credentials.json` and updates `settings.json` access_token/expiry; missing creds becomes unsupported skip. Expiry parsers updated for vault layout (Claude flat `auth.json`) and Gemini ErrNoAuthFile logic. Added unit tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:37:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-dw4","title":"Implement 'open' command to open account pages","description":"## Task
Add 'caam open' command to open the provider's account management page in the browser.

## Usage
```bash
caam open codex          # Open OpenAI account page
caam open claude work-1  # Open Anthropic account page (using work-1's browser config)
caam open gemini         # Open Google account page
```

## Account URLs
- Codex/OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/account
- Claude/Anthropic: https://console.anthropic.com/
- Gemini/Google: https://myaccount.google.com/ or https://aistudio.google.com/

## Browser Profile Integration
If a profile is specified and has browser config, open URL in that browser profile. This ensures you see the correct account's dashboard.

## Implementation
- Define account URLs per provider
- Use browser launcher (from browser epic) to open URL
- If no profile specified, use default browser

## Dependencies
- Should use browser launcher from browser epic
- Can work without it (fall back to default browser)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opens correct URL for each provider
- Uses browser profile if configured
- Falls back to default browser if no config","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.513290492-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:39:14.018599226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:39:14.018599226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented caam open command to open provider account pages with browser profile support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-dw4","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.533449267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-dw4","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:03.534802505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-ir7","title":"Project-profile association storage","description":"## Purpose
Create storage system for project-profile associations.

## Background
codex-pool pins conversations to accounts for context continuity.
For caam, we adapt this as "project-profile associations" - remembering which
profile a user prefers for each project directory.

This enables workflows like:
- Freelancers with multiple client accounts
- Work/personal separation by directory
- Team projects using team accounts

## Storage Format

### File Location
~/.caam/projects.json

### Schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "associations": {
    "/home/user/work/client-a": {
      "claude": "client-a@work.com",
      "codex": "work-main"
    },
    "/home/user/personal/blog": {
      "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
    }
  },
  "defaults": {
    "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
  }
}
```

## Technical Implementation

### Storage Interface
Create internal/project/store.go with ProjectStore struct that handles
loading, saving, and querying project associations.

### Path Matching
Support exact paths and glob patterns. Check parent directories if exact
match not found (inheritance).

### Atomic Writes
Use temp file + rename pattern for thread-safe updates (same pattern as
existing profile storage).

## Files to Create
- internal/project/store.go
- internal/project/store_test.go
- internal/project/matcher.go (path matching logic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Load and save project associations
- [ ] Query by directory path
- [ ] Parent directory inheritance works
- [ ] Atomic file writes
- [ ] Handle missing/corrupt file gracefully
- [ ] Version field for future migrations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:25:32.022502289-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:44.186281392-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:38:44.186281392-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented project-profile association storage (projects.json) with inheritance + glob matching, atomic writes, and tests.","comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"caam-ir7","author":"BlueBear","text":"Claimed by BlueBear. Implementing project association storage in internal/project (store + matcher + tests). No CLI wiring in this bead.","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:28:43Z"},{"id":3,"issue_id":"caam-ir7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes fix: project association store now canonicalizes provider keys/values on Load/Save (lowercase/trim, deterministic merge) to avoid nondeterministic Resolve and to ensure RemoveAssociation works with legacy/messy stored data. Added regression test.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:24:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ixy","title":"Detect and capture localhost redirect URLs from CLI output","description":"## Task
When running login commands (codex login, gemini), capture stdout and detect localhost URLs that need to be opened in a browser.

## Background
Codex CLI and Gemini CLI print a localhost URL during OAuth setup (e.g., http://localhost:1455/callback?code=...). Currently these auto-open in the default browser. We want to intercept and open in the configured browser profile instead.

## Implementation Approach
1. Wrap exec.Cmd with custom stdout handling
2. Use io.TeeReader or similar to both display output AND scan for URLs
3. Regex pattern: `http://localhost:[0-9]+[^ ]*`
4. When URL detected, suppress default browser open and use our launcher

## Challenges
- Some CLIs may use stderr for URLs
- Need to not break interactive features
- May need PTY for proper terminal handling
- Timing: URL may appear briefly before auto-open

## Alternative Approach
Set BROWSER env var to a wrapper script that calls back to caam. This is cleaner but requires a helper script.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can detect localhost URLs from codex login output
- Can detect localhost URLs from gemini output  
- Normal CLI output still visible to user
- Works with both stdout and stderr","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:31.574510149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:17:41.956736064-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:17:41.956736064-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented URL detection for OAuth flows: URLPattern regex, URLDetector io.Writer wrapper, OutputCapture for stdout/stderr, and BROWSER env var helper script generator.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ixy","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:31.58885101-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-j14","title":"Implement keyboard navigation and actions","description":"## Task
Wire up all keyboard shortcuts to their actions.

## Keybindings
- q: Quit
- ↑/↓: Navigate profiles
- Tab: Switch provider
- Enter: Run CLI with selected profile
- l: Login to selected profile
- o: Open account page in browser (requires browser epic)
- e: Edit profile
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter mode

## Implementation
- Handle key events in Update()
- Dispatch to appropriate actions
- Show confirmation dialogs for destructive actions

## Dependencies
- Requires browser epic for 'o' command
- Edit mode needs separate view state

## Acceptance Criteria
- All keybindings functional
- Delete requires confirmation
- Actions provide visual feedback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.395351148-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:45:17.918666626-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all keyboard navigation and actions: fixed 'l' key conflict, added Edit (e), Search/filter mode (/), updated help view and detail panel keybindings display","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.409454362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-ta7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.410828349-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-j14","depends_on_id":"caam-nmm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:52:40.411813644-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-j46","title":"Unit tests for internal/passthrough","description":"## Package: internal/passthrough

Symlink passthrough for isolated profiles.

## What to Test
- Passthrough directory list
- Symlink creation
- Target path resolution
- Missing target handling
- Existing symlink handling
- Symlink verification

## Approach
- Use t.TempDir() for test directories
- Create real symlinks
- Verify symlink targets
- Test edge cases (broken links, missing dirs)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T01:50:17.581338667-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T02:05:18.386219939-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T02:05:18.386219939-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive unit tests for internal/passthrough: DefaultPassthroughs, NewManager, NewManagerWithPaths, AddPassthrough, RemovePassthrough, SetupPassthroughs (symlink creation, nested paths, existing file replacement), VerifyPassthroughs (valid/invalid/broken symlinks), RemovePassthroughs, and full setup-verify-remove cycle.  {"id":"caam-jig","title":"Headless Authentication Support (Device Code Flow)","description":"# Headless Server Support

## Status: Core Features Complete!

The primary headless workflows are now fully functional:

### ✅ Completed

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Device auth setup | Works via native \`codex login --device-auth\` + \`caam backup\` |
| Hit-limit → switch → resume | Works via \`caam activate\` + native \`codex resume\` |
| Vault transfer | ✅ \`caam export/import\` implemented (caam-zha) |
| Documentation | ✅ AGENTS.md updated (caam-1r6) |
| AuthModeDeviceCode constant | ✅ Added (caam-hzu) |

### Remaining (P2/P3 - Profile Isolation Mode Only)

These are for the **advanced** Profile Isolation mode, not the primary workflow:

| ID | Task | Priority | Purpose |
|----|------|----------|---------|
| caam-v89 | --device-code CLI flag | P2 | For \`caam login\` in Profile Isolation mode |
| caam-ela | Codex provider support | P2 | Implementation for above |
| caam-0gy | Session resume | P3 | For Profile Isolation mode only |

## The Working Workflow

```bash
# SETUP (on headless server)
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam

# HIT LIMIT → SWITCH → RESUME
caam activate codex account2             # < 1 second!
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Native Codex
```

|----|--------|
| caam-1xv | Research complete - Claude lacks good headless option |
| caam-ai6 | Research complete - vault transfer is best for Gemini |
| caam-e79 | Removed - headless detection was over-engineering |
| caam-m5a | Removed - QR code was gold-plating |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:00.94446079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","close_reason":"Core headless workflows complete: device auth setup, hit-limit→switch→resume, vault transfer, and documentation.  {"id":"caam-ljt","title":"Data retention and automatic cleanup","description":"## Purpose
Implement automatic cleanup of old activity logs to prevent unbounded database growth.

## Background
codex-pool uses configurable retention:
```go
logRetention: 7 * 24 * time.Hour, // Keep logs for 7 days
```

caam should have similar configurable retention with sensible defaults.

## Configuration
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
analytics:
  retention_days: 90        # Keep detailed logs for 90 days
  aggregate_retention: 365  # Keep aggregates for 1 year
  cleanup_on_startup: true  # Run cleanup when caam starts
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/db/cleanup.go

// CleanupConfig holds retention settings
type CleanupConfig struct {
    DetailedRetentionDays   int
    AggregateRetentionDays  int
}

// DefaultCleanupConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultCleanupConfig() CleanupConfig {
    return CleanupConfig{
        DetailedRetentionDays:  90,
        AggregateRetentionDays: 365,
    }
}

// Cleanup removes old records
func (d *DB) Cleanup(cfg CleanupConfig) (deleted int, err error) {
    cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -cfg.DetailedRetentionDays)
    
    result, err := d.conn.Exec(`
        DELETE FROM activity_log 
        WHERE timestamp < ?
    `, cutoff)
    
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    deleted, _ = result.RowsAffected()
    
    // Optionally VACUUM to reclaim space
    if deleted > 1000 {
        _, _ = d.conn.Exec("VACUUM")
    }
    
    return int(deleted), nil
}

// ScheduledCleanup runs cleanup periodically
func (d *DB) ScheduledCleanup(interval time.Duration, cfg CleanupConfig) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    for range ticker.C {
        d.Cleanup(cfg)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Manual cleanup
caam cleanup --dry-run
caam cleanup --older-than 30

# View database stats
caam db stats
Database: ~/.caam/data/caam.db
Size: 2.3 MB
Activity logs: 15,234 entries
Oldest entry: 2025-09-15
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/db/cleanup.go
- internal/db/cleanup_test.go
- cmd/caam/cleanup.go (optional CLI command)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Old logs are deleted based on retention config
- [ ] Cleanup is idempotent and safe
- [ ] VACUUM runs after large deletions
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows what would be deleted
- [ ] Cleanup does not block normal operations
- [ ] Sensible defaults that work for most users","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:23:38.014094581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:26.283091054-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:26.283091054-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented data retention and automatic cleanup: added cleanup.go with retention logic, cleanup_test.go with comprehensive tests, cleanup CLI command (caam cleanup with --dry-run/--days/--quiet), and integrated CleanupOnStartup into TUI. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-fwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:29:12.362276729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-0gb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:32:09.546260125-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-m5a","title":"Display QR code for device auth URL (nice-to-have)","description":"# Display QR Code for Device Auth URL

## What

When using device code flow, display a QR code that users can scan with their phone to quickly reach the authorization URL.

## Why

QR codes provide faster mobile access:
- No typing long URLs on phones
- Works even when URL can't be copy-pasted (some SSH clients)
- Professional/polished UX
- Many CLI tools (gh, az) do this successfully

## Implementation

### Dependencies

Use a pure-Go QR code library to avoid external dependencies:
- github.com/mdp/qrterminal (MIT license, popular)
- github.com/skip2/go-qrcode (generates image, not terminal)

### File: internal/auth/qrcode.go

\`\`\`go
package auth

import (
    "io"
    "os"
    
    "github.com/mdp/qrterminal/v3"
    "golang.org/x/term"
)

// DisplayQRCode prints a QR code to the terminal if stdout is a TTY
func DisplayQRCode(url string, w io.Writer) {
    // Only show QR if we have a TTY (not piped/redirected)
    if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) {
        return
    }
    
    // Use ASCII mode for compatibility
    config := qrterminal.Config{
        Level:     qrterminal.L,
        Writer:    w,
        BlackChar: qrterminal.WHITE, // Inverted for dark terminals
        WhiteChar: qrterminal.BLACK,
        QuietZone: 1,
    }
    qrterminal.GenerateWithConfig(url, config)
}
\`\`\`

### Integration in device code flow

\`\`\`go
func (p *Provider) loginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile) error {
    fmt.Println("Starting device code authentication...")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // For Codex, we need to capture the output to get the code
    // Then display our enhanced version
    
    // Display QR code pointing to the auth URL
    authURL := "https://auth.openai.com/codex/device"
    auth.DisplayQRCode(authURL, os.Stdout)
    
    fmt.Println("")
    fmt.Printf("Open: %s\\n", authURL)
    fmt.Println("Or scan the QR code above with your phone")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // Run the actual codex login --device-auth
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "codex", "login", "--device-auth")
    // ...
}
\`\`\`

## Output Example

\`\`\`
Starting device code authentication...

  █▀▀▀▀▀█ ▀▀▀▀█▀ █▀▀▀▀▀█
  █ ███ █ █▀▄█ ▀ █ ███ █
  █ ▀▀▀ █ ▀▄▀ ▄█ █ ▀▀▀ █
  ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀ ▀ █ ▀ ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀
  ██▀▀▀▀▀ ▀█▄ ▄▀▄▀█▄▀▄██
  ...

Open: https://auth.openai.com/codex/device
Or scan the QR code above with your phone

Enter code: ABCD-EFGH
\`\`\`

## UX Considerations

1. **Terminal compatibility**: QR codes may not render well in all terminals
   - Detect terminal capabilities if possible
   - Provide --no-qr flag to disable

2. **Color inversion**: Dark vs light terminals need different rendering
   - Try to auto-detect (COLORFGBG env var)
   - Provide --qr-invert flag

3. **Size**: QR code shouldn't overwhelm the terminal
   - Keep it reasonably sized
   - Use minimal quiet zone

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] QR code displays for device auth URL
- [ ] Works in common terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, GNOME Terminal)
- [ ] Gracefully skipped if not a TTY
- [ ] --no-qr flag to disable
- [ ] URL still printed as text (accessibility)

## Testing

- Visual test in various terminals
- Test with screen reader (ensure URL is still readable)
- Test when stdout is piped (QR should not appear)

## Notes

This is a polish/UX enhancement. Core device code flow should work without this.
Consider deferring until after core device code is working.

## Dependencies

- caam-ela: Codex device code implementation
- caam-v89: CLI --device-code flag","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:39:18.382441283-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:45.861881769-05:00","close_reason":"Removed as gold-plating. Codex already displays the URL clearly. QR code adds dependency and complexity for marginal UX gain. Keep caam simple.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-ela","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:18.087310678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-v89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:40:23.188174111-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-nac","title":"Implement browser launcher abstraction","description":"## Task
Create internal/browser/browser.go with a Launcher interface and implementations for different browsers.

## Design
```go
type Launcher interface {
    // Open opens a URL in the configured browser profile
    Open(url string) error
    // Name returns the browser name for display
    Name() string
}

type Config struct {
    Command    string // Browser executable
    ProfileDir string // Profile directory or name
}
```

## Implementations Needed
1. ChromeLauncher - uses --profile-directory flag
2. FirefoxLauncher - uses -P flag for profile name
3. DefaultLauncher - uses OS default (open/xdg-open)

## Platform Considerations
- macOS: Chrome is at /Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
- Linux: Usually 'google-chrome' or 'chromium-browser'
- Windows: Complex paths, need registry lookup or common paths

## Rationale
Abstraction allows us to support multiple browsers and add new ones easily. The interface also makes testing straightforward.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launcher interface defined
- Chrome implementation works on macOS and Linux
- Firefox implementation works
- Default fallback works
- Unit tests for each implementation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.47883372-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:16:04.783685539-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented browser launcher abstraction with Launcher interface, ChromeLauncher (--profile-directory), FirefoxLauncher (-P), DefaultLauncher (xdg-open/open), and GenericLauncher. Includes platform-specific browser detection for macOS/Linux/Windows.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-nac","depends_on_id":"caam-4eg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:26.499689437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
